context: Live E2E verification on a REAL user-supplied generated sprite-sheet (Gemini-generated knight, 1408x768, 4 animation rows). Ran after swapping the registered live server to the build that includes the tool and reconnecting. Aseprite 1.3.17.2 / plugin 0.3.2.
input_prep: The source is a 'presentation' sheet (labels IDLE/RUN/ATTACK/HURT, frame boxes, BAKED gray-checker background, irregular per-row frame counts). Prepared a clean uniform strip from the IDLE row: cropped the 4-frame band and flood-filled the gray checker to transparent from the borders (interior silver armour preserved) -> idle_strip.png (704x168 = 4 x 176, ~37% opaque).
call: live_import_animation(filename=idle_strip.png, sheet={cols:4,rows:1}, width=48, height=48, auto_colors=20, palette_method=median_cut, fps=6, tag=idle, layer='Reference Anim')
verification: live_list_frames -> 4 frames @ 0.166s; live_list_tags -> tag 'idle' spanning 1-4; live_save_filmstrip -> 4 recognizable knight frames (silver winged helm, red plume, blue+gold-cross shield, skin face), ONE shared palette across all frames (no colour flicker), frame 2 keeps the open-mouth + 'z' sleep cue from the source. distinct_colors == palette size (20) => palette-legal by construction (downscale snaps to the shared palette).
field_note: First call used palette_method=frequency and the auto_palette degenerated to a 16-step near-black ramp (#000000..#101010): on a SMOOTH (anti-aliased, non-pixel-art) source the finely-graded dark regions dominate exact-colour frequency counting. median_cut/kmeans (area-weighted clustering) is the correct choice for photos / AI renders -- exactly what the tool's param doc recommends. The tool plumbing (slice + N frames + shared palette + tag + durations) was correct in both runs; only the extraction-method choice mattered.
verdict: live_import_animation VERIFIED end-to-end on a real generated knight sheet: sheet-slice -> 4 frames -> ONE shared palette -> tagged animation at the requested fps, palette-legal.
